ddls:
CREATE TABLE "loggers" (
  "logger_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each logger */,
  "logger_name" TEXT /* Name of the logger (e.g., root, x, x.y, security) */,
  "parent_logger_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent logger (if any) */,
  "additivity_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the logger inherits appenders from its parent */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the logger was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the logger was last updated */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the logger is used (e.g., development, production) */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Default log level for the logger (e.g., INFO, ERROR, DEBUG) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the logger's purpose */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the logger configuration */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the logger is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("logger_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_loggers_parent_logger_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_logger_id") REFERENCES loggers ("logger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "appenders" (
  "appender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each appender */,
  "appender_name" TEXT /* Name of the appender (e.g., A1, A-x1, A-sec) */,
  "appender_type" TEXT /* Type of the appender (e.g., File, Console, Database) */,
  "output_target" TEXT /* Target where the appender outputs logs (e.g., file path, database table) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the appender was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the appender was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the appender is currently active */,
  "max_file_size" TEXT /* Maximum file size for file-based appenders */,
  "backup_count" INTEGER /* Number of backup files to keep for file-based appenders */,
  "encoding" TEXT /* Encoding for the log output */,
  "buffer_size" TEXT /* Buffer size for the appender */,
  "is_async" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the appender operates asynchronously */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("appender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logger_appender_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "logger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the logger */,
  "appender_id" INTEGER /* ID of the appender */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the mapping was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the mapping was last updated */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the appender is the default appender for the logger */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the appender for the logger */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logger_appender_mapping_logger_id FOREIGN KEY ("logger_id") REFERENCES loggers ("logger_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logger_appender_mapping_appender_id FOREIGN KEY ("appender_id") REFERENCES appenders ("appender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_entries" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "logger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the logger that generated the log */,
  "appender_id" INTEGER /* ID of the appender that recorded the log */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the log (e.g., INFO, ERROR, DEBUG) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Content of the log message */,
  "log_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the log was generated */,
  "source_file" TEXT /* Source file where the log was generated */,
  "source_line" INTEGER /* Line number in the source file where the log was generated */,
  "thread_id" TEXT /* ID of the thread that generated the log */,
  "process_id" TEXT /* ID of the process that generated the log */,
  "log_context" TEXT /* Additional context information (e.g., user ID, session ID) */,
  "log_stack_trace" TEXT /* Stack trace if an error or exception occurs */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log entry (e.g., application, middleware) */,
  "log_tags" TEXT /* Tags for easier filtering and categorization of logs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_entries_appender_id FOREIGN KEY ("appender_id") REFERENCES appenders ("appender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_entries_logger_id FOREIGN KEY ("logger_id") REFERENCES loggers ("logger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_filters" (
  "filter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log filter */,
  "logger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the logger */,
  "filter_type" TEXT /* Type of the filter (e.g., Level, Pattern) */,
  "filter_expression" TEXT /* Expression used to filter logs */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the filter was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the filter was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the filter is currently active */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the filter's purpose */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the filter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("filter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_filters_logger_id FOREIGN KEY ("logger_id") REFERENCES loggers ("logger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log report */,
  "logger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the logger */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., Daily, Weekly) */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "generated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the report was generated */,
  "generated_by" TEXT /* User who generated the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV, HTML) */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., generated, pending, failed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the report's content */,
  "is_archived" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the report is archived */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_reports_logger_id FOREIGN KEY ("logger_id") REFERENCES loggers ("logger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, monitor) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user was last updated */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts */,
  "lockout_time" TEXT /* Time when the account is locked out due to failed login attempts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, developer, monitor) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the role is active */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role's responsibilities */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout, view, edit) */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the access event */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "access_action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view logger, edit appender) */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the access attempt was successful */,
  "response_code" TEXT /* HTTP response code for the access attempt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_trail" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit trail entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "action_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the action */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Details of the action performed */,
  "affected_logger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the logger affected by the action */,
  "affected_appender_id" INTEGER /* ID of the appender affected by the action */,
  "is_rollbackable" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the action can be rolled back */,
  "rollback_details" TEXT /* Details for rolling back the action */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the action was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trail_affected_appender_id FOREIGN KEY ("affected_appender_id") REFERENCES appenders ("appender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trail_affected_logger_id FOREIGN KEY ("affected_logger_id") REFERENCES loggers ("logger_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trail_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
